reasoning: L(OL)+ means L followed by one or more copies of OL; choose two copies to avoid duplicating LO+L.
(HO)+ requires at least one HO; choose one.
K(EK)*E is K then zero or more EK then E; choose zero EK.
(HAR+)+ is one or more of HA followed by one or more R; choose HA with one R once.
H(EH)+ is H then one or more EH; choose one EH.
ROT?FL is RO plus optional T plus FL; omit T.
TE(HE+)+ is TE then one or more HE+; take HE once with one E.
LAW*L is LA then zero or more W then L; choose one W.
MWA(HA)+ is MWA then one or more HA; choose one.
HE(HE)+ is HE then at least one HE; choose one.
LO+L is L then one or more O then L; choose two Os to differ from L(OL)+.
HAHA* is HA then H followed by zero or more As; choose zero extra As.
(AH)+A+ is one or more AH then one or more A; choose AH once and A once.
HA+ is H then at least one A; choose one A.
(JA)+ is one or more JA; choose one.
LOL*LO?(OL)?O+L → choose L* = 0, O? = absent, (OL)? = absent, O+ = O; yields LOLOL.
(A?HA)+(LO)?O* → one A?HA with A omitted gives HA; optional LO and any Os omitted.
